ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100043931) FILED BY DEFENDANT MAXIMUS FEDERAL SERVICES, INC.
PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION TO STRIKE (TRANSACTION ID # 62379351) FILED BY PLAINTIFF K, MARJORIE K, PRESTON

LAW AND MOTION 302, ANTHEM BLUE CROSS (PARTIALLY JOINED BY MAXIMUS FEDERAL SERVICES, INC.) DEMURS TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT'S FIRST (BREACH OF CONTRACT) AND SECOND (INSURANCE BAD FAITH) CAUSES OF ACTION. IT IS ADEQUATELY PLED THAT INSURANCE TERMS AND COVERAGES WERE MISREPRESENTED, CLAIMS AND APPEALS MISHANDLED AND CLAIMS UNREASONABLY DENIED. MARJORIE K. AND HER SON PRESTON MAY BOTH SUE SO LONG AS NO DOUBLE RECOVERY RESULTS. (SEE, E.G., LAUGHNER V. BRYNE (1993) 18 CAL.APP.4TH 904.) MARJORIE HAS STANDING TO SUE AS A PARTY TO THE INSURANCE CONTRACT AND PRESTON BECAUSE CLAIMS FOR HIS CARE WERE ALLEGEDLY WRONGFULLY DENIED. PRESTON IS NOW AN ADULT WITH THE APPARENT LEGAL CAPACITY TO SUE, SO THE COMPLAINT MAY BE SO AMENDED WITHIN 20 DAYS. THE DEMURRERS ARE OTHERWISE OVERRULED. THE ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS - THE SINGLE ORDER ADDRESSES THE COURT'S RULING ON ALL THREE MATTERS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: MTORREANO@SFTC.ORG. =(302/RBU)

Defendant Blue Cross Of California Dba Anthem Blue Cross'S Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Katie Spielman, Esq. of DL Law Group, 415.6378.5050, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Karen Braje, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP, 415.659.5948, appearing for the Defendant.

Defendant Blue Cross Of California Dba Anthem Blue Cross'S Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Anthem Blue Cross (partially joined by Maximus Federal Services, Inc.) demurs to the second amended complaint's first (breach of contract) and second (insurance bad faith) causes of action. It is adequately pled that insurance terms and coverages were misrepresented, claims and appeals mishandled and claims unreasonably denied. Marjorie K. and her son Preston may both sue so long as no double recovery results. (See, e.g., Laughner v. Bryne (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 904.) Marjorie has standing to sue as a party to the insurance contract and Preston because claims for his care were allegedly wrongfully denied. Preston is now an adult with the apparent legal capacity to sue, so the complaint may be so amended within 20 days. The demurrers are otherwise OVERRULED. The order was signed in open court. (see order for full details - the single order addresses the Court's ruling on all three matters)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)

Partial Joinder Of Defendant Maximus Federal Services, Inc. In Anthem Blue Cross'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint (Or In The Alternative Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings

MINI MINUTES:

Katie Spielman, Esq. of DL Law Group, 415.6378.5050, appearing for the Plaintiff;

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

Partial Joinder Of Defendant Maximus Federal Services, Inc. In Anthem Blue Cross'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint (Or In The Alternative Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Anthem Blue Cross (partially joined by Maximus Federal Services, Inc.) demurs to the second amended complaint's first (breach of contract) and second (insurance bad faith) causes of action. It is adequately pled that insurance terms and coverages were misrepresented, claims and appeals mishandled and claims unreasonably denied. Marjorie K. and her son Preston may both sue so long as no double recovery results. (See, e.g., Laughner v. Bryne (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 904.) Marjorie has standing to sue as a party to the insurance contract and Preston because claims for his care were allegedly wrongfully denied. Preston is now an adult with the apparent legal capacity to sue, so the complaint may be so amended within 20 days. The demurrers are otherwise OVERRULED. The order was signed in open court. (see order for full details - the single order addresses the Court's ruling on all three matters)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)

Defendant Blue Cross Of California Dba Anthem Blue Cross'S Motion To Strike Claims For And Reference To Punitive Damages And Attorney Fees From The Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Katie Spielman, Esq. of DL Law Group, 415.6378.5050, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Karen Braje, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP, 415.659.5948, appearing for the Defendant.

Defendant Blue Cross Of California Dba Anthem Blue Cross'S Motion To Strike Claims For And Reference To Punitive Damages And Attorney Fees From The Second Amended Complaint.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Anthem Blue Cross's motion to strike claimed punitive damages, treble damages and attorney fees is DENIED. It is adequately alleged that Anthem (1) acted fraudulently, maliciously, oppressively and in conscious disregard of rights, (2) violated Civil Code 3345 and (3) may be subject to an attorney fee award under, e.g., Brandt v. Sup. Ct. (1985) 37 Cal.3d 813.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)
